title: ziziphora granatensis
intro: Ziziphora granatensis, also known as Spanish lavender or mountain mint, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family. It is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean, where it has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds such as borneol and camphor, which have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it useful for treating respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions. Ziziphora granatensis can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or essential oil, and is often used to relieve colds, coughs, and sore throats.
